question: Are there any outliers in the Temperature and Luminosity relationship?

analysis.chart_type: Scatter plot with log scales and outlier annotation
analysis.design_rationale: A scatter plot is ideal for visualizing the direct relationship between Temperature and Luminosity, particularly for outlier detection in continuous, wide-ranging astronomical data. Applying log scales to Luminosity (which ranges from nearly zero to hundreds of thousands) and Temperature improves interpretability and reveals deviations from the main distribution more clearly. Outliers can be highlighted using statistical thresholds (e.g., points above 95th percentile in Luminosity or far from the main cloud).
analysis.target_insight: Visual identification and annotation of stars whose Luminosity vs. Temperature relationship is markedly different than the majority, empowering the viewer to spot extreme cases in the dataset.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Log scale for y-axis; Outlier points annotated with red border and labels; alpha=0.7 for main points

transformations: Apply np.log10 to df['Luminosity(L/Lo)'] for more even spacing
Compute z-score for df['Luminosity(L/Lo)'] to identify outliers (|z|>2.5)
Extract subset of stars whose Luminosity is above 2.5 std deviations from the mean
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Load data
 df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\192.csv")

# Compute z-score for Luminosity
 df['Luminosity_z'] = (df['Luminosity(L/Lo)'] - df['Luminosity(L/Lo)'].mean()) / df['Luminosity(L/Lo)'].std()

# Identify outliers
 df_outliers = df[df['Luminosity_z'].abs() > 2.5]
 df_main = df[df['Luminosity_z'].abs() <= 2.5]

# Create plot
 fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6))

# Scatter main points
 ax.scatter(df_main['Temperature (K)'], df_main['Luminosity(L/Lo)'], alpha=0.7, edgecolor="none", label="Normal range")

# Scatter outliers
 ax.scatter(df_outliers['Temperature (K)'], df_outliers['Luminosity(L/Lo)'], facecolor="none", edgecolor="red", s=60, label="Outliers")

# Annotate outliers
 for i, row in df_outliers.iterrows():
    ax.text(row['Temperature (K)'], row['Luminosity(L/Lo)'], "Outlier", color="red", fontsize=8, ha="right", va="bottom")

# Set log scale for y-axis
 ax.set_yscale("log")

# Axis labels and title
 ax.set_xlabel("Temperature (K)")
 ax.set_ylabel("Luminosity (L/Lo) (log scale)")
 ax.set_title("Scatter Plot of Temperature vs. Luminosity\nwith Outliers Highlighted")
 ax.legend()
 plt.tight_layout()
 plt.show()
